task_description: Fetch user data from REST API and update role field
reasoning_chain: Understand the goal: fetch user data, then update specific fields.
Identify required API endpoints: GET /users/{id} and PATCH /users/{id}.
Determine auth method: Bearer token in Authorization header.
Plan error handling: 404 (user not found), 401 (unauthorized), 422 (bad payload).
Execute fetch, validate response schema, build update payload, execute PATCH.
Verify update by re-fetching and comparing fields.

steps.description: Obtain Bearer token via OAuth2 client credentials flow
steps.reasoning: All subsequent API calls require authentication. Fetching the token first avoids repeated 401 errors.
steps.error_handling: Retry once on network timeout; raise ValueError on 401.

steps.description: GET /users/42 to retrieve current user record
steps.reasoning: We need the current state before updating to avoid overwriting unchanged fields.
steps.error_handling: Raise UserNotFoundError on 404; log and exit on 500.

steps.description: Construct PATCH payload with only changed fields
steps.reasoning: PATCH semantics require sending only modified fields to avoid race conditions.
steps.error_handling: Validate payload against schema before sending.

steps.reasoning: Apply the minimal update using PATCH to preserve unmodified fields server-side.
steps.error_handling: Retry on 429 with exponential backoff; raise on 422.

steps.description: Re-fetch user and assert updated field matches expected value
steps.reasoning: Verification closes the feedback loop and catches eventual-consistency issues.
steps.error_handling: Raise AssertionError if role does not match after update.
